content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

De-duplicated `.beads/issues.jsonl` serialization by adding a shared `write_bead_graph_issues` helper in `src/test_support/fixtures.rs`, switching `BeadGraphFixtureBuilder::write_into` to use it, and updating `ScenarioState::persist` in `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs` to call the same helper. Removed the copied scenario-only serializer structs and acceptance-criteria formatting logic from `e2e_fixtures.rs`, so initial fixture writes and post-mutation rewrites now share one canonical on-disk schema path.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the duplicated serialization paths in `src/test_support/fixtures.rs` and `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs` to confirm the mutation path was maintaining a second copy of the `.beads` JSON schema.
2. [Completed] Added a shared bead-graph writer helper in `src/test_support/fixtures.rs`, switched the fixture builder to use it, and rewired `ScenarioState::persist` to call the same helper while deleting the duplicated serializer definitions from `e2e_fixtures.rs`.
3. [Completed] Ran formatting and cargo validation on the refactor, then re-ran `nix build` on the final tree and recorded the lack of a clean completion as the remaining risk.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture_smoke_test --lib` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix build` was started on the final tree and reached the musl derivation build step, but no successful process exit was captured.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` was re-run on the final tree and progressed to building `/nix/store/0yaj10n93hc0mpvm05l951zjy3ab85dr-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but the `nix` client never returned a clean completion during observation. I did not mark the tree as Nix-validated.
